Title: Integrative transcriptome and proteome analysis reveals maize responses to Fusarium verticillioides infection inside the stalks

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defence against chitin-containing fungal pathogen infection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing of ZmCTA1 enhanced susceptibility to Fusarium verticillioides, supporting a positive role in maize defence metabolism.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: multi-omics identification; RT-qPCR; PRM; virus-induced gene silencing
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in maize stalks after Fusarium verticillioides infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCTA1 was identified as an infection-responsive candidate from integrated transcriptome and proteome analyses. 2. Its transcript/protein change was validated by RT-qPCR and PRM. 3. VIGS-mediated silencing increased disease susceptibility, indicating contribution to resistance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential resistance gene target for breeding maize with improved stalk rot resistance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on disease resistance rather than a nutritional end-product; no discrete nutrient metabolite directly quantified for ZmCTA1.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing of ZmWIP1 enhanced susceptibility to Fusarium verticillioides, indicating a positive role in resistance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: multi-omics identification; RT-qPCR; PRM; virus-induced gene silencing
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in maize stalks after Fusarium verticillioides infection
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Wound-induced Bowman-Birk inhibitor; serine protease inhibitor
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmWIP1 was strongly regulated after fungal infection in transcriptome/proteome datasets. 2. Expression trend was validated by RT-qPCR and PRM. 3. Gene silencing increased fungal susceptibility, supporting a defensive function.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful resistance-associated gene for breeding or engineering stalk rot resistance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although important for defense, it is not presented as a regulator of a defined nutrient metabolite.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipoxygenase (LOX) family; 9-LOX discussed in paper context
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: LOX-mediated oxylipin branch linked to salicylic-acid-dependent defense
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Fatty acid hydroperoxides / hydroperoxides leading to oxylipins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxygenation of linoleic acid to produce conjugated diene hydroperoxides, initiating oxylipin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Salicylic acid-associated defense state; oxylipin-related lipid peroxidation products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: fatty acid-derived oxylipin / phytohormone-associated defense metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes maize defense against Fusarium verticillioides infection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, roots, and stalk tissues during infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmLOX2 positively contributes to resistance; silencing increases stalk rot severity, reduces LOX activity, lowers SA accumulation, H2O2, MDA, lignin, and PR gene expression.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: integrated transcriptome/proteome analysis; RT-qPCR; PRM; VIGS knockdown; enzyme activity assay; SA/JA quantification; H2O2 and MDA assays; hormone pretreatment rescue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after Fusarium verticillioides infection in maize stalks; strongly induced by exogenous SA and weakly by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium verticillioides infection; exogenous salicylic acid; methyl jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmLOX2 was identified from integrated transcriptome and proteome analyses as an infection-responsive core candidate in linoleic acid metabolism. 2. Silencing ZmLOX2 increased disease severity in maize roots and stalks, demonstrating a positive role in resistance. 3. ZmLOX2 silencing reduced LOX activity, H2O2, MDA, free and total SA, and SA-responsive PR gene expression. 4. SA, but not MeJA, restored resistance in silenced plants, supporting action through an SA-mediated pathway rather than JA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for improving maize stalk rot resistance by maintaining or enhancing LOX2-dependent defense signaling; should be used with caution because the mechanism centers on defense metabolism rather than direct nutrient quality traits.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes defense metabolism, not nutritional biofortification. ZmLOX2 is treated as a core experimentally validated gene; other LOX genes were mainly validation/background comparisons. Recombinant ZmLOX2 was expressed in Escherichia coli for enzyme-related assays, but the applied species for phenotype was maize.
